metadata.description: Agent skills marketplace for Claude Code and Codex

plugins.name: how-plugins-work
plugins.description: Reference for plugin naming, skill resolution, Claude/Codex adapter sync, marketplace aliases, runtime caches, and plugin:skill invocation.

plugins.name: self-improvement
plugins.description: Routes user feedback about agent behavior to the strongest durable target: hooks, skills, project code, or instruction files.

plugins.name: git-discipline
plugins.description: Git workflow skills, repo-aware push policy, and commit/push hooks for agent sessions and direct CLI commits.

plugins.name: intervision
plugins.description: Bring another coding agent in as a peer to review work just done or just discussed. Claude hands work to Codex; Codex hands work to Claude. Intervision is peer consultation among equals, not supervision from above.

plugins.name: anger-management
plugins.description: Curse at your coding agent to capture a one-line friction note, then fix the real problem later. The curse commands (/fuck, /fucking, /fucked, /shit, /crap, /wtf, /bullshit) append one cheap line to a global log and move on; /anger-management:repair cools down (~22 min, a background headless agent investigates), judges whether there is one concrete recurring thing worth fixing, and routes it to /self-improvement (often by loosening a rule that overcorrected, not adding one), or changes nothing if there is no clear pattern. Modelled on Stripe's bad-day button and the constructed-emotion view of anger. Vocabulary avoids slurs and identity-targeted abuse. State is stored locally; the optional background investigation uses the configured runner.

plugins.name: autonomous
plugins.description: Keep an autonomous mission running across turns. A startup capability probe decides whether keep-alive machinery is needed: a cron heartbeat with exponential backoff, auto-stop, and wake/restore for an interactive session; nothing for a persistent process. The rover plugin's decision framework calls this layer at dispatch.

plugins.name: rover
plugins.description: Dispatch a rover at a task and stay back while it decides in the field: a SURVEY/DRIVE/INSPECT/STOW phase machine with a decide framework, pride and trim quality gates, verify evidence discipline, prepare for queuing missions across repos, and a stop communique. Asks the autonomous layer at startup whether it needs to keep itself alive. Depends on gurus for the INSPECT panel review.
